notes:
v0.3.2 — kills the em-dash bug for good.

Claude Haiku is great at most things, but it has a tic: roughly one in ten polished outputs would slip an em-dash (—) into the text. It's the single most common AI tell, and it broke the rule every Voquence mode prompt is supposed to follow.

v0.3.2 adds a hard guarantee in the Rust layer: em-dashes and en-dashes are stripped after Claude returns, replaced with a clean sentence break (". ") and the next word is capitalized. So "Your space is chaotic—your mind follows." becomes "Your space is chaotic. Your mind follows." Same meaning, no AI tell.

Four unit tests cover the common shapes so this doesn't regress.

No breaking changes. Same Settings, same hotkey, same keys. The polished output just sounds more like you now.
